what_it_does: Defines a React functional component HubBug that renders a top-left brand bug (ConceptV logo image) plus a hub switcher: a 'STAGE' label and a row of tab buttons built from a hubs array, marking the active one and calling onSwitch(id) on click. Attaches the component to window.HubBug.
observations: Component takes three props: hubs (array), activeHub (id), onSwitch (callback)
Renders an img from a relative path '../../assets/logos/conceptv-v-yellow.png' with alt 'ConceptV'
Maps hubs to <button> tabs keyed by h.id, conditionally adding an 'active' class when h.id === activeHub
Click handler is an inline arrow calling onSwitch(h.id)
Uses className strings tied to a 'vh-' prefixed CSS namespace (vh-bug, vh-bug-logo, vh-hubsw)
Final line assigns the function to window.HubBug rather than using module exports
standouts: window.HubBug = HubBug global attachment — the integration mechanism instead of ES modules
conventions: Follows a 'vh-' CSS class namespace shared across the virtual-hub kit
Babel-transpiled-in-browser JSX with global window attachment (matches the index.html loader pattern)
Relative asset path resolution from the component's directory depth (../../)
Breaks the claude-ds 'no second home / registry projection' charter: this is a standalone hand-written component with literals, not a CV_REGISTRY type
concerns: Hardcoded image path and 'STAGE' label are inline literals rather than tokenised/registered values
No prop validation or defaults; missing hubs would throw on .map
notes: A small presentational React component for the virtual-hub overlay's brand+hub-switcher, wired in via window.HubBug.
questions: Where does the hubs array and onSwitch handler originate (presumably HubApp.jsx)?
Is the 'vh-' styling defined in hub.css referenced by the kit's index.html?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the header comment 'top-left brand + hub switcher' accurately describes what the component renders.
apparent_themes: Presentational component composition for a tour/overlay UI
Global-window wiring for a Babel-in-browser prototype kit
intention_signals: Aims to give the buyer-side virtual-hub overlay a persistent top-left identity element and a way to switch between hubs/stages, kept as a simple standalone JSX building block in the UI kit.
contribution: Contributes the brand-bug + hub/stage-switcher UI pattern for the virtual-hub overlay — a reusable top-left header element with active-tab switching that a fused system would keep as the hub navigation affordance.
summary_for_embedding: A React component (HubBug) rendering a ConceptV logo bug and a STAGE hub-switcher tab row, attached to window.HubBug for a Babel-in-browser virtual-hub UI kit.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the virtual-hub overlay's persistent top-left brand identity and hub/stage switching control.
